purpose: Relational learning moment—students see that different containers can represent the same mathematical structure.
visual: Context Visualizations (Mode 2) (×2). Split screen: Left shows concrete (bags), Right shows concrete (boxes). 2 groups of 5 items each. Same structure in both. [RELATIONAL COMPARISON] designation.
guide: "Look at these two. On the left—bags. On the right—boxes."
guide_2: "Different containers. But notice what's the SAME."
visual_3: Both visuals show "2 groups" label appear
visual_4: Items in one bag highlight, then items in one box highlight
guide_5: "Different containers—bags or boxes—but the SAME structure. Both are 2 groups of 5. Both build the same expression: 2 times 5."
guide_6: "Equal groups work the same way no matter what holds them."
divider: This is relational teaching—building conceptual understanding that structure transcends visual form.
design_note: This is the RELATIONAL phase's core moment—showing the pattern exists across different visual forms. Prevents "3×4 only means bags" misconception.
